reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary:
Author responded to Steven Rostedt's concern that calling ftrace_free_filter() twice could cause issues, explaining that it sets the ->*_hash values to EMPTY_HASH and thus wouldn't lead to problems.

Author acknowledged that the error handling in perf_event_alloc() error cleanup path needs to be restructured, and agreed to do so in v2.

Author is unsure about the specific code being referenced by Sashiko, and asks if they are looking at a different tree than upstream Linus.

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch fixes a warning that occurs when trying to unregister an ftrace function that was never successfully registered. The fix involves checking if the ftrace_ops is enabled before attempting to unregister it, and if not, simply freeing the filter and returning success. This prevents a WARN_ON condition from being triggered during error cleanup in perf_event_alloc(). The patch modifies the perf_ftrace_function_unregister() function to include this check.

reviews.author: Masami (Google)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where perf_ftrace_function_unregister() is called on an event whose ftrace_ops registration failed, and suggested adding a check for FTRACE_OPS_FL_ENABLED before unregistering

Reviewer Masami noted that the commit being reviewed is too old and not used anymore, requesting further investigation from Sashiko.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, 22 May 2026 16:39:41 -0400
Rik van Riel <riel@surriel.com> wrote:

---

You can see the baseline info if you expand the collapsed triangle.
Anyway, it said:

linux-trace/HEAD (70575e77839f4c5337ce2653b39b86bb365a870e)

So that is linux-trace/master.

commit 70575e77839f4c5337ce2653b39b86bb365a870e (linux-trace/master)
Merge: 7bc6e90d7aa4 a43ae8057cc1
Author: Linus Torvalds <torvalds@linux-foundation.org>
Date:   Fri Sep 30 09:41:34 2022 -0700

    Merge tag 'for_linus' of git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/mst/vhost


Hmm, this is too old. And linux-trace/master is not used anymore.

Reported to Sashiko.

https://github.com/sashiko-dev/sashiko/issues/218

Thank you,

-- 
Masami Hiramatsu (Google) <mhiramat@kernel.org>

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch fixes a warning that occurs when trying to unregister an ftrace function that was never successfully registered. The fix involves checking if the ftrace_ops is enabled before attempting to unregister it, and if not, simply freeing the filter and returning success. This prevents a WARN_ON condition from being triggered during error cleanup in perf_event_alloc(). The patch modifies the perf_ftrace_function_unregister() function to include this check.

reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the patch does not check if the ftrace_ops is enabled before calling unregister_ftrace_function(), which can lead to a WARN_ON in __unregister_ftrace_function()

reviewer suggested deleting a branch in the patch
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, 25 May 2026 14:39:47 +0900
Masami Hiramatsu (Google) <mhiramat@kernel.org> wrote:

---

Hmm, I probably should delete that branch.

Thanks,

-- Steve

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a warning that occurs when trying to unregister an ftrace function that was never successfully registered. The fix involves checking if the ftrace_ops is enabled before attempting to unregister it, and if not, simply freeing the filter and returning success. This prevents a WARN_ON condition from being triggered during error cleanup in perf_event_alloc(). The patch modifies the perf_ftrace_function_unregister() function to include this check.
